name: ExtensionRegulatedAuthorization_Regulator
title: R5: The regulatory authority or authorizing body granting the authorization (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `RegulatedAuthorization.regulator` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`RegulatedAuthorization.regulator` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Organization)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `RegulatedAuthorization.regulator` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Organization)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `RegulatedAuthorization.regulator` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `RegulatedAuthorization` is not mapped.
Element `RegulatedAuthorization.regulator` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The regulatory authority or authorizing body granting the authorization (new)
element.comment:
Element `RegulatedAuthorization.regulator` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `RegulatedAuthorization` is not mapped.
Element `RegulatedAuthorization.regulator` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: The regulatory authority or authorizing body granting the authorization
element.definition: The regulatory authority or authorizing body granting the authorization. For example, European Medicines Agency (EMA), Food and Drug Administration (FDA), Health Canada (HC), etc.
